behaviorsAudited: adds a PTY scenario that launches workbench mode with embedded Neovim BUFFER enabled
drives insert, edit, write, undo, dirty quit refusal, force quit, search navigation, visual yank paste, named register basics, macro replay, resize, mouse isolation, and process cleanup
verifies file contents on disk after Neovim write operations
verifies killing the TUI leaves no Neovim child process alive
verifies missing Neovim uses inline fallback with a clear visible reason
keeps advanced Vim command behavior owned by embedded Neovim in the PTY flow instead of the inline Vim layer

edgeCasesAudited: embedded Neovim undo changes file contents after write and before later edits continue
dirty colon q keeps the workbench running and shows the Neovim refusal
colon q bang exits BUFFER without writing edited text
resizing during insert mode preserves typed text, cursor position, and Neovim grid visibility
mouse wheel over BUFFER does not move the workspace tree rows
mouse click over BUFFER moves the Neovim cursor without moving the workspace tree rows
a forced TUI kill while Neovim has dirty edits cleans up the embedded child process without writing dirty text

adds a PTY scenario that launches workbe: TODO.md:199-210 requires PTY end-to-end BUFFER coverage, including launch, edit, fallback, resize, mouse, and cleanup.
runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:15-23 sets workbench mode, BUFFER provider auto-selection, and test auth environment.
runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:30-49 starts the TUI, opens BUFFER, waits for embedded Neovim, and asserts a child nvim process exists.
runtime/scripts/check-tui-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:9-18 runs the Neovim-filtered PTY scenarios with AGENC_TUI_WORKBENCH enabled.
runtime/package.json:33-34 exposes the TUI e2e runner and the workbench-buffer-neovim wrapper command.
drives insert, edit, write, undo, dirty: runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:51-79 covers insert, write, and undo before a second write.
runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:86-118 covers visual yank/paste, named register, and macro replay inputs.
runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:120-181 covers resize, cursor-position probing, BUFFER mouse wheel isolation, and BUFFER mouse click routing.
runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:183-255 covers advanced save assertions, search navigation, dirty :q refusal, :q! discard, and child cleanup.
runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/122-workbench-buffer-neovim-kill-cleanup.mjs:47-61 covers dirty mid-edit TUI kill cleanup.
verifies file contents on disk after Neo: runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:64-79 reads target.txt after writes and asserts the original marker is written then removed by undo.
runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:189-208 reads target.txt after advanced commands and asserts macro, register, resize, click routing, and visual paste effects.
runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:251-254 reads target.txt after :q! and asserts dirty text was not written.
verifies killing the TUI leaves no Neovi: runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/122-workbench-buffer-neovim-kill-cleanup.mjs:13-20 configures the kill-cleanup PTY scenario with workbench, provider, and test auth token.
runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/122-workbench-buffer-neovim-kill-cleanup.mjs:32-46 opens BUFFER and asserts a child nvim process exists before kill.

runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/122-workbench-buffer-neovim-kill-cleanup.mjs:47-61 creates dirty text, kills the TUI, waits for child pids to disappear, and asserts dirty text was not written.
runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/helpers/workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:28-47 finds descendant Neovim child pids, and lines 49-62 wait for those pids to disappear.
verifies missing Neovim uses inline fall: runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/121-workbench-buffer-neovim-missing-fallback.mjs:12-19 configures embedded Neovim mode with test auth.

runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/121-workbench-buffer-neovim-missing-fallback.mjs:25-50 points AGENC_BUFFER_NVIM at a missing binary, waits for visible fallback text, and asserts no nvim child spawned.
keeps advanced Vim command behavior owne: TODO.md:107-115 states commands like :w, :q, :q!, search, visual mode, macros, registers, and undo are owned by Neovim, not reinterpreted by AgenC.
runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:42-49 requires the embedded Neovim screen and live child process before advanced Vim commands are driven.
runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:86-118 and 210-224 drive visual, register, macro, and search commands through the embedded session.

embedded Neovim undo changes file conten: runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:64-79 writes E2E_MARK, runs undo, writes again, and asserts E2E_MARK is absent from disk before later edits continue at lines 86-118.
dirty colon q keeps the workbench runnin: runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:228-240 inserts DIRTY_MARK, sends :q, waits for E37/unsaved refusal text, and asserts BUFFER remains visible.
colon q bang exits BUFFER without writin: runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:243-255 sends :q!, reads target.txt, asserts DIRTY_MARK was not written, and waits for the embedded child process to exit.
resizing during insert mode preserves ty: runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:120-132 resizes during insert mode, waits for RESIZE_MARK_AFTER, and asserts normal mode plus preserved text remain in the frame.

runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:133-143 writes getline('.') and col('.') from Neovim after resize and asserts both the current line and cursor column remain on RESIZE_MARK_AFTER.
runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:199-201 asserts the preserved resize text was written to disk.
mouse wheel over BUFFER does not move th: runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:146-159 snapshots workspace rows before and after BUFFER wheel input and fails if they differ.
mouse click over BUFFER moves the Neovim: runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:161-181 snapshots workspace rows before and after click input and then inserts CLICK_ROUTE_ at the clicked Neovim line.
runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:202-205 asserts the saved alpha line contains CLICK_ROUTE_.
a forced TUI kill while Neovim has dirty: runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/122-workbench-buffer-neovim-kill-cleanup.mjs:47-61 creates KILL_DIRTY_MARK, verifies it is visible before kill, kills the TUI, waits for Neovim child pids to disappear, and asserts KILL_DIRTY_MARK is absent from disk.
